title: In memory, March 2013
summary: Rose Lorraine Wolcoff, 100 Rose Lorraine Wolcoff passed away Feb. 14 at Harborview Manor Assisted Living Home in Wasilla, Alaska. Ms. Wolcoff was born Feb. 13, 1913, in Chignik, Alaska and celebrated her 100th birthday in 2013. She is survived by her daughters, Melva Withers, Margaret Smith, Ruth and her husband Lee Hilty, and Mary Wolcoff; son, Paul Wolcoff Jr. and his wife, Donna; 19 grandchildren, 40 great-gran...

title: Shareholder information meetings and annual meeting
summary: Meeting attendance is limited to CIRI shareholders and their immediate family members (i.e. spouse, children and/or stock custodian). title: CIRI dividend and Elders’ payment news
summary: CIRI will mail or directly deposit its first quarter 2013 dividend of $8.41 per share on March 29. Shareholders who own 100 shares of stock will receive $841. Payments from the CIRI Elders’ Settlement Trust were mailed or directly deposited on March 8 in the amount of $450. The deadline for signing...

title: 2013-2014 Education Resources Handbook available online
summary: The CIRI Foundation’s 2013-2014 Education Resources Handbook is now available online at www.thecirifoundation.org . title: Resource revenue payment distributions
summary: Section 7(i) of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA) requires each regional corporation to share 70 percent of its net resource revenues received from ANCSA lands with the other regional corporations. summary: NYO athlete participating in the One-Hand Reach contest. title: Cook Inlet Housing Authority Part of the solution
summary: Rendering of CIHA’s Coronado Park Senior Villa planned for Eagle River. Alaska is witnessing a population boom among senior citizens and some fear senior housing is not keeping pace. title: CIRI spotlight:  Joel Isaak
summary: Joel Isaak Joel Isaak started taking art seriously when he was in junior high school in his hometown of Soldotna, Alaska. title: A major investor, AMP Capital, joins CIRI-backed wind venture
summary: AMP Ltd., an Australian pension company, provided a major capital investment in wind energy owner Capistrano Wind Partners LLC. The partnership acquires and operates large-scale wind energy projects nationwide. Capistrano was formed last year by CIRI, Teachers Insurance and Annuity Association and C...

title: A word from the president, March 2013
summary: By: Sophie Minich, CIRI president and chief executive officer Earlier this month I had the honor of participating in the signing ceremony to convey more than 200,000 acres of land to CIRI village corporations. This transaction, which you can read more about in this issue of the Raven’s Circle, is monumental for a number of reasons. First and foremost, it brings to a close the long process to finally convey the last of the major land entitlements to the CIRI...

title: Kenaitze progress on the Dena’ina Wellness Center
summary: View of the Dena’ina Wellness Center construction show Lobby/Gathering Area in the foreground with 2nd floor curved balcony and roofing insulation staged at the upper roof beyond. The construction of the Kenaitze Indian Tribe’s Dena’ina Wellness Center remains on schedule for completion next winter....

title: 12(b) land selections finalized
summary: Ninilchik Natives Association President and CEO Greg Encelewski and CIRI President and CEO Sophie Minich at the March 1 ceremony, signing the quick claim deed to convey Ninilchik’s 12(b) land selections. In a long-awaited and emotional ceremony, CIRI and its village corporations came together March 1 for an historic land transfer. CIRI President and CEO Sophie Minich signed the deed transfers for more than 230,000 acres that now become village corporation land more than 40 years after passage of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA). The transfer of the 12(b)...
